text: Does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system decompose a Gaussian wave packet v0=4*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25) (with u0=0) into a soliton train by T=6.0 (>=2 well-separated peaks, each amp>=0.8, mass drift <8%)?

method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives (no dealiasing/filter) + explicit RK4 on the full coupled system u_t = -3 u u_x - 3 (v^2)_x - v_xxx and v_t = -6 v v_x - v_xxx - (u v)_x
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline mandates a baseline before any IMEX/dealiasing. Positive bank entries (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass, kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation) confirm spectral derivatives are appropriate for the v_xxx term but suggest IMEX-CN as the eventual upgrade -- I keep IMEX in reserve for E2. Negative entries (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup, kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs, kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation) tell me to avoid IFRK4 entirely and to NOT use central FD on the advective terms. I therefore choose pseudospectral derivatives (smooth IC + periodic domain make spectral safe relative to central FD) + plain explicit RK4 with dt=1e-4 satisfying the linear RK4 stability bound k_max^3*dt~1.95 < 2.78. E1 is the simplest baseline that even has a chance, and progressive-complexity discipline forbids stacking IMEX+dealiasing at E1.

diagnostics.first_warning: overflow in multiply at u*v and v*v
diagnostics.interpretation: Explicit RK4 + spectral derivatives without dealiasing blew up before t=0.5 (well before T=6). The amplitude A=4 implies max|6v|+max|v^2|~24+16=40 -- far above the bank's amp=1.5 case (kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary). Linear RK4 stability for k_max^3*dt is OK but the explicit nonlinear stiffness combined with aliasing of cubic terms (3v^2 contribution) drove the overflow.

rationale: NaN by t=0.45 means E1 cannot answer Q1. As predicted by bank entries (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup explains why explicit-only fails on dispersive terms; kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup and kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary explain why amplitude 4 blows up where amp 1.5 worked). Failure mode is clearly identified: dispersive stiffness + nonlinear stiffness + aliasing.

Single-component change for E2: replace explicit RK4 with IMEX (Crank-Nicolson on the linear stiff dispersive operator v_xxx + the linear part of u_t = -v_xxx, explicit on the nonlinear terms). Spectral derivatives stay. NO dealiasing added at this step -- progressive-complexity allows ONE change per E. Bank entries kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass and kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation back this direction (without dealiasing for now; dealiasing reserved for E3 if F2 shows aliasing artifacts).

method: Fourier pseudospectral + IMEX-Crank-Nicolson (CN on linear v_xxx coupling in both u_t and v_t equations, explicit Euler on nonlinear terms -3uu_x, -3(v^2)_x, -6vv_x, -(uv)_x). NO dealiasing.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: only the time integrator changed (explicit RK4 -> IMEX-CN). Positive entries kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass (KdV amp 2, dt=5e-4 stable) and kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation (Gardner amp 1.5, dt=5e-4 stable) directly motivate this method. Negative entries kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup and kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup justify abandoning explicit-only treatment of the dispersive term. dt=5e-4 reproduces the bank's stable dt. NO dealiasing yet -- E3 will add it if F2 shows aliasing-driven spurious peaks (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing). Pseudospectral derivatives unchanged.

diagnostics.interpretation: IMEX-CN removed the linear stiffness of v_xxx (as predicted by kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass), but the explicit nonlinear part still blew up by t=0.5. Root cause: amp=4 implies max|6v + (uv)|~24-32 and the cubic-like product v^2 + uv accumulates aliasing energy without a 2/3 filter (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing). Per kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary, doubling A roughly 2.5x's the nonlinear CFL; amp=4 vs G2's amp=1.5 means the nonlinear-CFL budget shrinks by ~factor 5. dt=5e-4 (G2 budget) is therefore at most 1e-4 at A=4.

rationale: Failure mode is NOT dispersive stiffness (IMEX-CN already cured that) but rather (a) un-dealiased nonlinear aliasing accumulating at amp 4 and (b) dt too large for the nonlinear CFL at amp 4. E3 must add 2/3-dealiasing (the bank-validated single fix at G2) and reduce dt commensurately.

E3 layers ONE more component over E2: add 2/3-rule dealiasing on the nonlinear-product FFTs (v^2 and uv and u*u_x, v*v_x). Also reduce dt from 5e-4 to 1e-4 to satisfy the amp=4 nonlinear-CFL implied by kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary (max|6A+1.5A^2|/max|6*1.5+1.5*1.5^2|=37.5/12.4~3 so dt~5e-4/3~1.7e-4; use 1e-4 with margin). Spectral pseudospectral + IMEX-CN unchanged. This is exactly the kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass + kb-gardner-G2 stack, the established positive recipe in the bank.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + IMEX-Crank-Nicolson (CN on linear v_xxx coupling, explicit Euler on nonlinear) + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear-product FFT (inputs masked before pointwise multiply, outputs re-masked). dt reduced from 5e-4 to 1e-4 to satisfy nonlinear-CFL at amp=4.
bank_use_rationale: Single component added over E2: 2/3-rule dealiasing on all nonlinear products, paired with the dt reduction implied by the bank's amplitude-CFL scaling (kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary). Positive entries kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass and kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation establish that IMEX-CN + 2/3 dealiasing + spectral derivatives is the validated baseline for KdV-class problems. Negative entries kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing pinpointed dealiasing as the next single-component fix. Soliton-train phenomenon target requires accurate amplitude and peak count (kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic), which the bank's preferred stack is designed to provide.

diagnostics.note: Initial run at dt=1e-4 NaN'd at t=1.2; same-method bug-fix re-tune to dt=2e-5 reached t=3.72 then NaN; second re-tune to dt=1e-5 ran cleanly to T=6. The dt re-tunes are same-method parameter bug-fixes per progressive-complexity (reducing dt is the listed single allowable single-component refinement). Method unchanged through these re-tunes.

rationale: Phenomenon target met: (a) mass(v) drift 0.0% << 8% target, (b) >=2 well-separated peaks each amp >= 0.8: 12 peaks above 0.8 with several well-separated (peak-to-peak spacing >= 2 dx ~ 0.234 units) -- e.g. peaks at x=-13.6, -10.9, -7.4, -4.6, -2.5, +1.5, +4.0, +7.4, +11.9, +14.5 are all well-separated (>= ~1.5 unit gaps). The Gaussian wave packet has clearly decomposed into a soliton train, consistent with KdV-type inverse-scattering dispersive decomposition.
F3 has useful_self_assessment=true and the phenomenon target is met (mass drift 0% << 8%; >=12 peaks above 0.8). Stop early; no further iteration needed.
